title: From insider trading in France to an American cell phone: How Spain tracked down former prime minister Zapatero
subhead: Judicial cooperation among several countries, including France and Switzerland, has helped uncover an international network relying on 'opaque financial channels,' which were allegedly used, among other things, to pay the former Socialist leader.

title: US officials announce details of agreement with Iran
European Parliament's approval of new GMOs marks turning point for agriculture sector
Italian historian Carlo Ginzburg, founding figure of microhistory, has died
subhead: Through his research on the 16th and 17th-century Inquisition trials in Europe, he initiated a profound upheaval in the field of historical scholarship. He died on June 17 at the age of 87.

title: US Fed chair Warsh says 'jobs data' moving in 'good direction'
subhead: Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that labor markets were stable, noting that 'trends matter more than data points.' Policymakers, at Warsh's first meeting in charge of the central bank, said economic activity was 'expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty that owes, in part, to the conflict in the Middle East.'

title: 2026 World Cup: International press hails long-distance duel between 'giants' Messi and Mbappé
subhead: With outstanding performances in their opening matches, Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi captured the attention of media outlets around the world, who saw the beginnings of a new showdown during this World Cup.

title: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
subhead: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.

title: Trump, buoyed by his deal with Iran, shows renewed interest in Ukraine at G7 summit
French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
Death of 11-year-old Lyhanna reignites debate over the funding of France's justice system

EU lawmakers approve migration reform allowing for creation of  'return hubs'
Polish PM says killing of Russian artist critical of Putin has signs of political assassination
Repeat fines targeting young Black and Arab men in France have severe consequences, report finds
Amid the ruins of southern Lebanon, Hezbollah declares victory over Israel
Musk’s AI, Grok, was used in strikes in Iran, reveals Pentagon
Lionel Messi scores a hat-trick, ties the all-time World Cup scoring record and leads Argentina to victory
After the US-Iran deal, Israel revels in its military might but finds itself at a strategic impasse
subhead: While Israel aimed to eliminate the 'existential threat' posed by the Iranian regime, it was sidelined from the memorandum of understanding reached Sunday between the US and Iran. Meanwhile, its military now risks losing its freedom of action in Lebanon.

title: In China's Qinhuangdao, architect Moshe Safdie's futuristic complex reimagines urban living
subhead: The utopian 'Hai Bi Tai,' a 16-story residential complex with 1,800 units, most of which offer views of the sky, the city and the Bohai Sea, still has half its apartments standing empty due to a real estate crisis.

title: Weakened at home, Macron counts on 'his' last G7 to boost his diplomatic record
subhead: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

title: Economic and xenophobic: The double threat posed by the Rassemblement National
'In the US, the contradictions in the alliance between the tech world and the MAGA movement can no longer be suppressed'
'Italy has no need for a Xenia Fedorova, because pro-Russian discourse is relayed through more diffuse and socially accepted channels'
